data.description:
## Why
The shared `impact = mean(severity attributes)` dilutes a genuinely high-severity plan finding below
the 0.70 bar (a critical finding averaged with low axes lands ~0.60-0.69). The mean is the wrong aggregator.

## What
For the plan-review gate, replace the mean with severity-first **MAX** over the axis contributions plus
a **hard override**: any of {AC-unverifiable, DoD-uncertifiable, undecomposed (from the deterministic
signal), divergent-implementation} floors impact to >= 0.85 regardless of the other axes (FMEA Action-
Priority / Chromium P0 pattern). Add a **detection amplifier** (silent-vs-self-revealing, DoD-uncertifiable):
escalate what a downstream gate cannot catch. Dispatch `impact()` per-gate off the shared `decide.py`
kernel, mirroring how `threshold_for` already dispatches per-gate.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` (per-gate `impact()`), the plan-review verify prompt (emit the
  axis sub-answers), and the per-gate dispatch wiring. Depends on the deterministic G5 signal child.
- No code-review changes here (code-review impact is a separate child).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `decide.py` computes a plan-review impact = MAX(axes) with the hard-override list, replacing the
      mean for this gate; code-review path is untouched by this child.
- [ ] Detection amplifier applied (silent / DoD-uncertifiable escalate).
- [ ] Per-gate dispatch in place (plan uses the new `impact()`), byte-compatible signed-verdict shape.
- [ ] Offline replay: previously-stranded band findings clear the bar; the recorded verdict set stays sane.
data.title: Plan-review impact redesign: severity-first MAX + hard override + detection amplifier
